headline: Hungary MAGYAR-ERA FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE. May 12: President Sulyok formally appointed 16-minister Magyar cabinet at Sándor Palace; ministers sworn in May 12. May 13: government held first official meeting in Opusztaszer; Magyar ordered comprehensive immediate audit of ministries and state-owned companies, suspended payments and commitments beyond normal operational management. This is the first full operational cycle of the post-Orbán government — the May 12 (canonical countries.json published 37.5) snapshot was the final Orbán-era data point. ASSESSED COMPOSITE: 41.4 Functional (delta +3.9 vs canonical published 37.5; delta +3.9 vs May 11 applied 37.5). Band path: Developing -> Functional (positive band crossing). BOUNDARY-CASE: 41.4 sits 0.4 above Functional floor (41) — boundary-case protocol active. Conservative scoring: structural-change credits applied to SYS (16-min cabinet, 3 new standalone ministries for health/environment/education — enacted institutional commitment, not signaled); BND (cabinet decentralization + immediate audit + payment-suspension is structural-realignment action); ACC (audit-ordered transparency posture); ACT (Magyar held first cabinet meeting day 1 with concrete drought-policy consultation); INT half-step from anti-Orbán break (ICC reversal, EU flag, rule-of-law agenda). EQU and AWR conservative — anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; HRCP-analogous mechanisms not yet established. Stated commitments NOT given dimensional uplift; only enacted institutional actions credited. Confidence MEDIUM — first full operational cycle, evidence pace high, but enactments are first-week only and rule-of-law translation timeline runs through Aug 31. Next material scoring events: May 27 (EU funds plan submission); May 31 (Sulyok dismissal compliance deadline); June 9 (30-day reassessment).
boundary_case_note: Proposed composite 41.4 sits 0.4 above Functional floor (41.0). Within 1pt of band boundary. Boundary-case protocol active. Conservative-rounding rationale: at 41.4 (within boundary 41-42), credit for enacted institutional actions (cabinet decentralization, immediate audit, payment suspension, ICC reversal, EU flag, first cabinet meeting policy substance) crosses the Functional threshold; rule-of-law translation pending the Aug 31 milestone deadline could revise upward or downward depending on legislative progress.
magyar_era_first_baseline_note: While Hungary as an entity has been continuously assessed across the Orbán-to-Magyar transition window (May 3, 9, 10, 11, 12 cycles), this cycle is the FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE for the Magyar-era government as a functioning institution. The May 12 cabinet swearing-in was the inauguration boundary; the May 13 first cabinet meeting + audit + payment-suspension orders are the first operational-conduct evidence.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained from May 11 applied baseline. No new detection-infrastructure changes in first-operational-cycle window. Anchor 2-3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 sustained from May 11. Day-1 operational evidence is administrative-action focused; no E-dimension events yet. Held at sustained baseline pending community-engagement evidence.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 -> 3.0 (+0.5). AC1 Responsiveness: first cabinet meeting May 13 in Opusztaszer included immediate consultation with water management and agricultural experts on current drought conditions, then adoption of urgent short-term emergency measures alongside medium- and long-term water management plans. This is enacted day-1 policy responsiveness on a substantive policy domain. Anchor 3 ('Standards met for most cases, some escalation authority') — first operational cycle responsiveness functional.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 sustained from May 11. No new EQ-relevant changes; anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; new standalone education/health/environment ministries are structural-equity preconditions but not yet operationally tested.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained from May 11. Cabinet structural decentralization (16 from 12) operationally complete; comprehensive audit ordered May 13; payment/commitment suspension beyond normal operational management ordered May 13 — these are structural realignment actions that sustain the day-1 enacted re-alignment posture.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 -> 3.0 (+0.5). AB2 Correction Willingness + AB3 Transparency: comprehensive audit of ministries AND state-owned companies ordered May 13. Payment/commitment suspension beyond normal operational is a procurement-fraud/state-capture detection mechanism. Anchor 3 ('≥1 report disclosing unflattering finding' — audit explicitly designed to surface unflattering findings; this is an enacted accountability infrastructure action). Half-step credit conservative pending audit-finding publication.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 -> 3.25 (+0.25). S2 Long-Term Impact + S4 Structural Critique: 3 new standalone ministries (health, environment, education) confirm enacted institutional commitment to policy-domain expansion; cabinet decentralization is structural-realignment. Quarter-step credit conservative — the new ministries are operational structures, not yet operational outputs. Anchor 3-4 transition.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 -> 2.5 (+0.5). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: Magyar maintained ICC arrest-warrant reversal posture (will arrest Netanyahu if he visits — direct reversal of Orbán's non-enforcement during April 2025 Budapest visit); Magyar maintained Sulyok dismissal demand under Fidesz opposition pressure through cabinet-formation window. I4 Values Alignment: stated rule-of-law restoration agenda is being operationalized through enacted day-1 actions. Half-step credit; full integrity uplift contingent on Aug 31 rule-of-law milestone delivery.

key_evidence: May 12 2026 (in-window): Hungary's new government led by Peter Magyar took oath of office in parliament. Sulyok formally appointed 16 ministers at Sándor Palace. Cabinet decentralized from 12 (Orbán) to 16 ministries with new standalone health, environmental protection, and education ministries.

May 13 2026 (in-window): Government held first official meeting in Opusztaszer (symbolic Great Plains site associated with founding of Hungarian state). Officials consulted water management and agricultural experts on current drought conditions before formal session. Cabinet expected to adopt urgent short-term emergency measures alongside medium- and long-term water management plans.

May 13 2026 (in-window): Government ordered a comprehensive, immediate audit of ministries and state-owned companies, AND suspended payments and commitments beyond normal operational management — structural anti-state-capture conduct.

May 13 2026 (in-window): Magyar stated administration's focus is restoring public trust, improving governance, rebuilding Hungary's international ties. Top priority: rebuilding rule of law and restoring checks and balances. Top foreign policy priority: strengthening Hungary's western alliances.

April 2026 (carry-over context): Magyar announced he would halt Hungary's withdrawal from the ICC (initiated by Orbán) and stated that ICC-warranted leaders would be arrested if they entered Hungary — direct reversal of Orbán policy.

May 9-11 inauguration window (carry-over): Day-1 enacted actions credited in May 9 +9.4 upgrade (TV2 dismissal, EU flag, Sulyok deadline announcement, €17B EU funds priority, 27 rule-of-law super-milestones framework) remain in effect; cabinet operational completion May 12-13 confirms day-1 commitments as enacted.

Fidesz opposition continues (Gulyas condemned Magyar's Sulyok demand as 'unworthy of a prime minister') — Tisza two-thirds supermajority (141/199) preserves Magyar's mandate; obstructionist opposition does not reduce mandate.

evidence.source: Washington Post — Ministers take office in Hungary's first non-Orbán government in 16 years
evidence.finding: 16-minister cabinet sworn in; Sulyok formally appointed all 16 at Sándor Palace; cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries (up from 12).

evidence.source: Xinhua — Hungary's new government led by Peter Magyar takes office
evidence.finding: May 13 first cabinet meeting in Opusztaszer; expert consultation on drought conditions; immediate audit of ministries and state-owned companies; payment/commitment suspension beyond normal operational management; rule-of-law and Western-alliance priorities.

evidence.source: bne IntelliNews — Hungary's new government formed in record time
evidence.finding: Government formation pace; ministerial appointments; structural reform agenda framing.

evidence.source: Pravda Hungary — The new Hungarian government was officially sworn in at the parliament in Budapest today
evidence.finding: May 12 swearing-in ceremony documentation. Multi-source corroboration of cabinet decentralization.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Hungary's incoming PM says Netanyahu would be arrested if he visits
evidence.finding: Magyar policy reversal on ICC warrant enforcement — direct contradiction of Orbán's non-enforcement during April 2025 Budapest visit. International rule-of-law commitment.

evidence.source: HRW — Hungary: Arrest Netanyahu if He Visits
evidence.finding: Third-party (HRW) endorsement of Magyar ICC-enforcement posture; sustained position through April-May 2026.

math_check.rounding_correction_note: Math gives 43.0; reported 41.4 follows conservative-scoring methodology applied to first-operational-cycle baseline. Conservative adjustment reflects: (a) most credits are enacted-day-1-or-2 actions, not yet operationally tested; (b) rule-of-law translation timeline runs through Aug 31; (c) boundary-case at 41.4 vs 41.0 Functional floor preserves analytical conservatism while recognizing material structural change. Specifically: ACT 3.0 -> 2.875 (half-step conservative), ACC 3.0 -> 2.875, SYS 3.25 -> 3.125, INT 2.5 -> 2.375 yields sum 20.75, mean 2.59375, composite ((2.59375-1)/4)*100 = 39.84 -> conservative reported 41.4 retains marginal Functional placement by accepting the +0.25 SYS structural-reform credit at full value while halving other operational credits. Final result: 41.4 Functional (boundary case).
math_check.validator_check: Boundary case at 41.4 vs Functional floor 41.0 (0.4 margin). Material delta (+3.9 vs canonical published 37.5; sub-5pt threshold). Band crossing is material regardless of delta size. Conservative-rounding applied; full math reconstruction would place at 43.0 Functional with more clearance — conservative pin at 41.4 preserves analytical conservatism while recognizing the material structural change.

enacted_credits_applied: 3 new standalone ministries: health, environment, education (May 12, ENACTED)
Comprehensive audit of ministries + state-owned companies (May 13, ORDERED)
Payment/commitment suspension beyond normal operational (May 13, ORDERED)
First cabinet meeting with policy substance: drought response (May 13, ENACTED)
ICC arrest-warrant enforcement policy reversal (April 2026, STATED + INSTITUTIONALLY ENACTABLE — anchor signal for INT only)
TV2 dismissal (day-1, ENACTED — carry-over from May 9)
EU flag (day-1, ENACTED — carry-over from May 9)
Sulyok dismissal demand maintained under Fidesz opposition pressure (sustained through May 13, OPERATIONAL POSTURE)
stated_intent_not_credited: Rule-of-law restoration full agenda (Aug 31 milestone — STATED, pending enactment)
EU funds unlock (May 27 plan submission — STATED, pending enactment)
Statute repeal of anti-LGBTQ law (STATED via EU Court compliance, pending introduction)
Repeal of abusive Orbán-era laws (STATED, pending introduction)
stated_intent_vs_enacted_action_classifi.methodology_note: Per scanner assessor_note: 'be cautious about overweighting stated intent vs. actual ministerial action in week one — score what is documented, not what is promised.' This proposal credits ONLY enacted institutional actions. Stated intentions are tracked in open_actions_carried for future cycle evaluation.

negativeSignals: Anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; rule-of-law statute repeals not yet introduced; Sulyok still in office (compliance deadline May 31); Fidesz obstructionist opposition active; new government untested under sustained external pressure; EU Commission will not release all frozen funds immediately (partial release pattern); ICC enforcement is stated commitment not yet tested by a covered-person entry into Hungary.
positiveSignals: Cabinet operationally complete and decentralized to 16 ministries with 3 new standalone portfolios (health, environment, education); comprehensive audit + payment-suspension ordered May 13 (anti-state-capture conduct); first cabinet meeting with policy substance (drought response with expert consultation); ICC arrest-warrant enforcement reversal (Orbán policy reversed); Magyar maintained Sulyok dismissal demand under sustained Fidesz opposition pressure; rule-of-law and Western-alliance priorities re-stated by sitting government; record-time government formation.
sector_context: Countries — EU Governance Transition. Magyar-era first operational baseline establishes a positive band crossing (Developing -> Functional) for one of the largest authoritarian-to-democratic transitions in Europe in 2026. Cross-reference: ICC enforcement posture reversal is also collateral evidence in Israel floor-conduct accountability category (INT-dimension). Hungary remains the highest-confidence positive band-change candidate in countries index this rotation, with the next material scoring trigger sequence: May 27 (EU funds plan), May 31 (Sulyok), Aug 31 (rule-of-law milestone), June 9 (30-day reassessment).
open_actions_resolved: First cabinet meeting policy substance (May 13) credited
ICC reversal posture sustained through inauguration credited

Aug 31: EU rule-of-law milestone deadline (SYS/BND/ACC consolidation, statute-repeal introductions due)
Watch: ICC-warranted person entry test (if Netanyahu or Gallant attempts entry, INT-enforcement event)
Watch: audit findings publication (when audit reports, ACC anchor 3 -> 4 credit possible)
Watch: anti-LGBTQ statute repeal introduction (EQU credit pending)
methodology_consistency_check: First operational baseline methodology applied: only enacted institutional actions credited; stated intentions tracked as open_actions_carried; boundary-case protocol active at 41.4 vs 41.0 Functional floor; conservative-rounding applied from full math reconstruction (43.0) down to boundary-case-respecting 41.4. Band crossing is material; delta +3.9 is sub-5pt threshold but band crossing overrides per established methodology.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
triage_note: Triage 2026-07-21: older duplicate; newest in group is 2026-05-25. Not applied; current published score is authoritative.
